name: Fats Domino
abstract: Antoine Caliste "Fats" Domino Jr. (February 26, 1928 – October 24, 2017) was an American singer-songwriter and pianist.

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: You’re born 1925, so you’re already 30 years old, Bill Haley and the Comets and Elvis and Little Richard and Fats Domino and all that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Brunious
about_quotes.evidence: Even Fats Domino [sings “I’m Walking.] You see? So it’s all based within that, that whole New Orleans concept is based within that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Brunious interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: I used to listen to a lot of rhythm & blues you know, Fats Domino and Chuck Berry... And I loved that stuff
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: we had to do “Blueberry Hill” — Fats Domino, they’d say in Quebec.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, I worked with Fats Domino down in New Orleans...in the Dew Drop. ... Yeah. I made some records with him, too.

about_quotes.speaker: Cosimo Matassa
about_quotes.evidence: And he also was tough to do a session with... Except Fats Domino. He, I saw he may have to come up with thousands and thousands of dollars. But he knew what Fats was worth.
